---
title: 'ViT: An Image is Worth 16×16 Words'
description: 'Treating image patches as tokens: split into patches, embed, add a CLS
  token + positional embeddings, and run a standard Transformer encoder.'
categories:
- Vision · Transformers
---

Paper: [arXiv:2010.11929](https://arxiv.org/abs/2010.11929)

## Architecture

![ViT: image split into patches, linearly embedded with a CLS token and positional embeddings, fed to a Transformer encoder](/images/notes/vit-1.png)

## Code

**Patch & position embedding** — split the image into non-overlapping patches via a strided conv, prepend a learnable CLS token, and add positional embeddings.

```python
class PatchEmbedding(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, img_size, patch_size, in_channels, embed_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.patch_size = patch_size
        # Each conv kernel covers exactly one (patch_size × patch_size) region → embed_dim vector
        self.proj = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, embed_dim, kernel_size=patch_size, stride=patch_size)
        num_patches = (img_size // patch_size) ** 2
        # Learnable CLS token representing the whole image
        self.cls_token = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, 1, embed_dim))
        # Positional embeddings: 1 (CLS) + num_patches positions
        self.pos_embed = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(1, 1 + num_patches, embed_dim))

    def forward(self, x):                      # x: (B, C, H, W)
        B = x.size(0)
        x = self.proj(x)                       # (B, embed_dim, H/P, W/P)
        x = x.flatten(2).transpose(1, 2)       # (B, num_patches, embed_dim)
        cls_token = self.cls_token.expand(B, -1, -1)
        x = torch.cat((cls_token, x), dim=1)   # (B, 1 + num_patches, embed_dim)
        x = x + self.pos_embed
        return x
```

**MLP block** — two linear layers with a GELU activation and dropout.

```python
class MLP(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, hidden_features, dropout_rate):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc_1 = nn.Linear(in_features, hidden_features)
        self.fc_2 = nn.Linear(hidden_features, in_features)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(p=dropout_rate)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.dropout(F.gelu(self.fc_1(x)))
        x = self.dropout(self.fc_2(x))
        return x
```

**Transformer encoder layer** — pre-norm multi-head self-attention + MLP, each with a residual connection.

```python
class TransformerEncoderLayer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, embed_dim, num_heads, mlp_dim, dropout_rate):
        super().__init__()
        self.norm_1 = nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim)
        self.attn = nn.MultiheadAttention(embed_dim, num_heads, dropout=dropout_rate, batch_first=True)
        self.norm_2 = nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim)
        self.mlp = MLP(embed_dim, mlp_dim, dropout_rate)

    def forward(self, x):
        norm_x = self.norm_1(x)
        attn_out, _ = self.attn(norm_x, norm_x, norm_x)   # Q = K = V
        x = x + attn_out                                  # residual
        norm_x = self.norm_2(x)
        x = x + self.mlp(norm_x)                          # residual
        return x
```

**Full model** — patch-embed, stack of encoder layers, final norm, and a classification head on the CLS token.

```python
class VisionTransformer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, img_size, patch_size, in_channels, num_classes,
                 embed_dim, depth, num_heads, mlp_dim, dropout_rate):
        super().__init__()
        self.patch_embed = PatchEmbedding(img_size, patch_size, in_channels, embed_dim)
        self.encoder = nn.Sequential(
            *[TransformerEncoderLayer(embed_dim, num_heads, mlp_dim, dropout_rate) for _ in range(depth)]
        )
        self.norm = nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim)
        self.head = nn.Linear(embed_dim, num_classes)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.patch_embed(x)
        x = self.encoder(x)
        x = self.norm(x)
        cls_token = x[:, 0, :]          # CLS token as the image representation
        return self.head(cls_token)
```
